overview.hero_desc: An integral solution that merges the versatility of **Artificial Intelligence** with the infallibility of **Precision Engines** for total control of materials and production.
overview.sync_desc: The Agent constantly monitors Gmail. Any changes in AI rules or Canopy inventory are instantly reflected in the next automatic execution.
overview.security_desc: The system implements a robust security architecture to protect the integrity of industrial data.

security_items.title: User Roles (RBAC)
security_items.text: Permission differentiation between ADMIN, SUPERVISOR, and CONSULTOR to restrict critical actions.

security_items.title: Data Encryption
security_items.text: Use of irreversible hashing algorithms to protect access credentials (Zero-Knowledge).

security_items.title: Auditing
security_items.text: Tracking of changes in rules and inventory movements linked to each user.

tech_items.label: Frontend
tech_items.detail: Reactive UI with Tailwind CSS and Framer Motion.

tech_items.label: Backend
tech_items.value: REST API on Express with Cloud architecture.
tech_items.detail: REST API on Express with Cloud architecture.

modules_items.title: Material Handler
modules_items.description: Intelligent management of materials and general orders.
modules_items.details: This module uses Artificial Intelligence to make order reading flexible. It allows the user to define rules in human language that Gemini translates into filtering logic, achieving proactive automation that notifies via WhatsApp and Email about stock requirements.

modules_items.title: Canopy
modules_items.description: Precision engine for fabric stock control.
modules_items.details: High-fidelity deterministic module. Unlike AI, this engine 'measures' the PDF pixel by pixel to extract critical production data with 0% interpretation error. It's the vital tool for cross-referencing required vs. available fabric yardage in real inventory.

developer.title: Project Developer
developer.name: Otoniel Berroa
developer.desc: Architect and lead developer of this automation ecosystem, focused on optimizing industrial processes through cutting-edge technology.

canopy.title: Canopy Analyzer
canopy.subtitle: Process production PDFs to verify stock and register models.
canopy.uploadDesc: Upload the production PDF to analyze Jobs and verify stock.

safety.title: 📘 Double Hook Crane Handling
safety.subtitle: Safety protocol for Aluminum Bundles
safety.personal:
• Requires **2 operators**: Crane Operator and Helper / Signaler
• Constant communication at all times
safety.inspection:
• Crane functioning correctly
• Hooks in good condition
• Slings without damage
• Steel cable without broken wires
• Area free of people and obstacles
safety.prep:
• Identify bundle length and mark attachment points.
• **If over 20 ft**: Remove wood or materials protruding underneath.
safety.slings:
• Not going to top rack → Use **10 ft**
• Going to top rack → Use **8 ft or 6 ft** (prevents collisions)
**Special rule for top rack**: Remove intermediate wood before insertion for better control.
safety.hooks_gt20: One hook at **13 ft** (center) + Another at **1 ft** from the end.
safety.hooks_le20: One hook at **11 ft** (center) + Another at **1 ft** from the end.
safety.move:
• Lift hooks **evenly** and verify leveling.
• Movement **slow and controlled**, without sudden moves.
• Maintain constant communication with the signaler.
safety.save:
1. Attach both hooks evenly to the bundle, using corresponding FT (**11 ft or 13 ft** and **1 ft** from the end).
2. Lift the bundle straight, keeping it level.
3. Align the bundle with the rack or cart before introducing it.
4. Slowly introduce the end where **NOT** marked at 1 ft.
5. Advance slowly until the center sling (**11 ft or 13 ft**) touches the rack.
6. When reaching the end, lower the bundle until that end is supported on the cart.
7. With the end already supported, release the hook attached to the rack/cart.
8. With the other hook, move the bundle inward in a controlled manner.
9. When the bundle touches the end, release the second hook, always verifying stability.
10. With the **forklift**, insert the bundle always leaving enough space to be able to grab it again at the time of removal.
safety.remove:
1. Pull the bundle out approximately **2 ft** using the forklift.
2. Attach the hook furthest out to begin extraction.
3. Once the first hook is secured, lift the bundle slightly to remove it from the rack.
4. Withdraw the bundle slowly using that hook, until the cart inside the rack reaches the output limit.
5. When the cart reaches the limit, attach the **second hook**.
6. Lift the bundle until it completely detaches from the cart and is level.
7. With both hooks secured, move the bundle outward in a controlled manner.
8. Once outside the rack, continue with slow movements and proceed to lower it slowly into a safe position.
safety.prevention:
• Keep distance
• Do not stand under the load
• Daily inspection
safety.gold_rules:
• Do not operate alone
• No unbalanced loads
• Constant communication
safety.checks:
• Crane
• Hooks
• Slings
• Steel Cable
• Area
• Communication
safety.failure_msg: If there is a failure: **DO NOT operate and report immediately**.
